nodes.label: Q: На основі всіх post-ADR документів сформуй docs/implementation-roadmap.md
nodes.norm_label: q: на основі всіх post-adr документів сформуи docs/implementation-roadmap.md

nodes.label: Operational Guardrails
nodes.rationale: Technology choices must account for operations, restore ownership, correction workflows, audit preservation, migrations, tests, backups, and long-term maintenance rather than favoring a framework first.

nodes.label: Data Architecture Guardrails
nodes.rationale: The data design should model domain behavior, transaction rules, auditability, migrations, and backup expectations before finalizing tables or database technology.

nodes.label: Observability Guardrails
nodes.rationale: Operational design must keep business truth separate from runtime logs, protect sensitive data, test restore paths, and avoid heavy tooling unless the risk profile justifies it.

nodes.label: System Interaction Guardrails
nodes.rationale: Interaction architecture should place business rules outside UI components, define side effects and audit records before endpoint shape, and avoid distributed services before operational need is clear.

nodes.label: Architecture Research Options Markdown Brief
nodes.rationale: Editable source-of-truth research brief for the BodyLife CRM v1 architecture shortlist.

nodes.label: Architecture Research Options HTML Preview
nodes.rationale: Rendered companion view that links back to the Markdown source.

nodes.label: BodyLife CRM V1
nodes.rationale: V1 replaces paper and partial Excel accounting for clients, memberships, visits, cash payments, freezes, history, and daily reporting.

nodes.label: Recommended V1 Architecture
nodes.rationale: Shortlist favors modular monolith, hybrid server-rendered UI, separate business audit, and no v1 migration/offline-first.

nodes.label: Hybrid Server-Rendered UI
nodes.rationale: Server-rendered pages with interactive search, quick actions, and warnings for reception UX.

nodes.label: Business Audit History
nodes.rationale: Separate append-only business history for disputes.

nodes.label: Membership Domain Model
nodes.rationale: Largest domain risk is membership rules, frequent negative visits, and explaining how negatives were closed.

nodes.label: Negative Visits
nodes.rationale: Negative balance is a core workflow, not an edge case.

nodes.label: Comparative Gym, Membership, CRM, POS, and ERP Sources
nodes.norm_label: comparative gym, membership, crm, pos, and erp sources

nodes.label: MDN SPA and Progressive Web Apps Sources
nodes.norm_label: mdn spa and progressive web apps sources

nodes.label: Internal Hosted Web App for One Gym
nodes.rationale: Simple owner/admin hosted web app, paper fallback, no offline-first, native app, portal, or multi-tenant SaaS in v1.
nodes.norm_label: internal hosted web app for one gym

nodes.label: Modular Monolith
nodes.rationale: One deploy and one transactional system keep the core workflow atomic and membership rules centralized.

nodes.label: Hybrid Server-Rendered UI
nodes.rationale: Server-rendered pages with interactive islands only for reception-critical search, warnings, quick actions, and submit guards.

nodes.label: Membership Rule Ownership by Business Modules
nodes.rationale: Memberships owns formulas; UI, reports, visits, payments, freezes, and non-working days use public commands/queries.
nodes.norm_label: membership rule ownership by business modules

nodes.label: Hybrid Membership Model
nodes.rationale: Source facts plus centralized derived state explain and recalculate active status, remaining visits, negatives, dates, extensions, and warnings.

nodes.label: Append-Only Business Audit Separate from Technical Logs
nodes.rationale: Owner-readable audit explains business actions and corrections; technical logs remain separate for debugging and operations.
nodes.norm_label: append-only business audit separate from technical logs

nodes.label: Reports Query Layer over Canonical Records
nodes.rationale: Reports read visits, payments, Memberships queries, and audit; snapshots/read models are not v1 source of truth.
nodes.norm_label: reports query layer over canonical records

nodes.label: Clients/Search Identity Ownership
nodes.rationale: Clients/Search owns card, name, phone, last-four search, current-card uniqueness, duplicate warnings, and scanner boundary.

nodes.label: Provider-Managed Backups with Restore Check
nodes.rationale: Managed backups are accepted only with documented restore runbook, restore rehearsal, checklist, retention, RPO, and RTO expectations.

nodes.label: Manual Backfill and Paper Fallback Entries
nodes.rationale: No full import; active clients and outage records enter through domain commands with validation, recalculation, audit, and occurred_at/recorded_at.
nodes.norm_label: manual backfill and paper fallback entries

nodes.label: Editable MembershipType Catalog with Immutable Issued Snapshot
nodes.rationale: Owner edits future sales catalog; issued memberships keep copied historical type, duration, visit limit, and price.
nodes.norm_label: editable membershiptype catalog with immutable issued snapshot

nodes.label: Owner, Named Admin, and Shared Reception/Admin Accounts
nodes.rationale: Daily work stays practical while audit honestly records named account versus shared session and dangerous corrections stay controlled.
nodes.norm_label: owner, named admin, and shared reception/admin accounts

nodes.label: Client Self-Service Guardrail
nodes.rationale: No client portal/auth/API-first v1, but domain/application membership logic remains reusable for future client-safe read-only views.

nodes.norm_label: accepted adr package for bodylife crm

nodes.rationale: Accept source facts, controlled recalculation, inclusive end date, explicit negative closure, union extension days, and audit-triggered corrections.
